title: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu visits troops in southern Lebanon. (File/@IsraeliPM)

snapshot.title: In south Lebanon, Netanyahu says Israel will stay as long as Hezbollah ‘threatens us’
snapshot.description: JERUSALEM: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu visited troops in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, vowing that his country’s forces would stay in the area as long as Iran-backed Hezbollah remained a “threat.” His comments came after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement under US sponsorship last week to pave the way for peace between them and disarm Hezbollah. The
snapshot.author: AFP
snapshot.pageTitle: In south Lebanon, Netanyahu says Israel will stay as long as Hezbollah ‘threatens us’ | Arab News
